meta_title: For Freelancers: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to leverage portfolio building, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship faster and impress your clients today.
content.answer_block: When debugging stalls your progress, pivot to portfolio building. By documenting your fixes and refactoring broken logic into reusable modules, you transform technical debt into high-value assets. Using Antigravity to generate documentation and Claude Code to iterate, you turn every 'can't debug' moment into a feature for your professional portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and the error log is a graveyard of broken promises. You are a freelancer, and time is money—yet you are stuck in a rabbit hole of syntax errors and unhandled exceptions. The frustration isn't just about the code; it is about the paralyzing fear that your client will realize you are stuck.

But here is the secret: every bug is an opportunity for a architectural case study. By shifting your mindset from 'fixing' to 'building a portfolio', you stop seeing errors as failures and start seeing them as the foundation of your expertise. We are going to use AI-native tools to turn your debugging sessions into a showcase of your technical mastery.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Stop guessing. Use Claude Code to map the stack trace and identify the exact module causing the regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this error log and identify the specific architectural pattern causing the failure. Propose a more robust, decoupled refactoring approach.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file ./src/error_module.ts --task 'Identify the root cause of this runtime exception and suggest a fix'

steps.title: Refactor for Reusability
steps.description: Don't just patch the bug; turn the logic into a clean, testable utility function that you can feature in your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this logic into a clean, documented utility function. Include JSDoc comments and explain the design pattern used.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/utils.ts --instruction 'Apply the refactored logic and add comprehensive documentation'

steps.title: Automate the Test Suite
steps.description: Prove your code works. A strong portfolio requires evidence of reliability through automated tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for this module using Jest. Include edge cases and error handling scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create and run Jest unit tests for the newly refactored module'

steps.title: Generate Portfolio Case Study
steps.description: Extract the 'How' and 'Why' of your fix to create a compelling project post that attracts future clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a 200-word professional case study snippet explaining this technical challenge, the solution, and the performance improvement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Summarize the technical implementation of this feature for a portfolio case study'

steps.title: Deploy to Showcase
steps.description: Push your polished code to a public repository so clients can verify your work quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md file that highlights the architectural benefits of this module and instructs users on how to integrate it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit README.md --instruction 'Append the new project summary and usage instructions'

pro_tips: Always commit your 'broken' code as a separate branch before fixing it; it makes for a great 'Before/After' visual in your portfolio.
Use Claude Code to generate 'ADRs' (Architecture Decision Records) for every major bug fix; clients love seeing structured thinking.
Don't just fix the code—annotate the PR with why you chose that specific pattern, showing you understand the trade-offs.
If a bug takes longer than 2 hours to fix, build a small 'utility library' around it to justify the time spent as a product asset.
Keep a private 'Snippets' repo where you push every unique fix you make; it becomes your personal search engine for future client work.
content.next_steps: Once your code is clean and documented, create a dedicated 'Projects' page on your portfolio site, link your GitHub repository, and write a brief LinkedIn post sharing the architectural lesson you learned during this build.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs slow down your career growth. Turn your technical challenges into your greatest assets and start shipping code that proves your value to every potential client. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
